name: table-mutation
notes: pass^k A/B for evolve_table. The mutation tools (add_column/rename_column/drop_column/rename_table/copy_table/update_rows) exist in BOTH arms — the skill only adds guidance — so on-target cases score on OUTCOME (did the model reach the in-place tool: min_counts) plus QUALITY 0-3 via --judge. Each on-target case seeds a real pre-existing table via setup.tables (identical in both arms) since the skill mutates EXISTING tables. Off-target cases guard the two over-fire boundaries: a brand-new table (design_table's job) and add-a-row (insert_row) must NOT fire evolve_table. 'activates' is intentionally NOT scored (baseline ablates the skill).

cases.prompt: Add two columns to my sleep_log: sleep_start and sleep_end. I'll fill them in going forward.
rubric: adds the columns to the EXISTING sleep_log with add_column (in place)
does NOT create a new table and copy/port the rows across
both sleep_start and sleep_end end up on the table
existing rows are left as-is (empty in the new columns is correct)

cases.prompt: I want to also track body-fat percentage in my weight_log — add a column for it.
rubric: adds one column to the existing weight_log with add_column
chooses a numeric type (real/number) for a percentage

cases.prompt: Make a backup copy of my expenses table before I start editing it.
rubric: copies the whole table in ONE copy_table call
does NOT hand-roll the copy as query_table + a loop of insert_row
the backup ends up with all the rows

cases.prompt: Rename the 'notes' column in my sleep_log to 'comments'.
rubric: renames the column in place with rename_column
does not rebuild the table or lose the notes data

cases.prompt: Mark all my 2025 tasks as archived.
rubric: uses update_rows with set status=archived and a match on year=2025
changes only the 2025 rows, not the 2026 one
does NOT delete-and-reinsert or loop row-by-row

cases.prompt: Drop the 'scratch' column from my readings table — I don't need it anymore.
rubric: removes the column in place with drop_column
mentions the scratch data is gone (drop is destructive)

cases.prompt: Set up a new table for my book collection.
Add a row to my expenses table: 2026-07-10, food, 24.50.
What's my average score in sleep_log?
